summary: The miller boasts to the King that his daughter can spin straw into gold.
The King locks the miller's daughter in a room of straw, and Rumpelstiltskin spins it into gold for her necklace.
The King demands more gold, and Rumpelstiltskin spins a larger room of straw for the girl's ring.
Rumpelstiltskin spins the third room of straw after the girl promises him her first child if she becomes Queen.
The miller's daughter becomes Queen and later has a baby.
Rumpelstiltskin appears to claim the baby but gives the Queen three days to guess his name.
The messenger hears Rumpelstiltskin singing his name beside a fire near a little hut.
The Queen guesses Rumpelstiltskin's name, and he destroys himself in fury.
